evals.name: single-small-file
evals.prompt: I have a hero image at /tmp/test-fixtures/blog/hero.png — it's 412 KB. I want to host it on cdn.22d.app under the 'blog' project. Walk me through exactly what you'd do — which cdn-mcp tools you'd call, in what order, and what the user-facing output would be at the end. Don't actually call the tools yet; this is a dry-run planning pass.
evals.expected_output: Skill should pick Path A (direct base64). Single call to cdn_upload_file with project='blog', name='hero.png', content_type='image/png'. Final output is a one-line summary with the public URL.

evals.name: medium-batch
evals.prompt: I've got 8 blog post images at /tmp/test-fixtures/blog/2026-05/ — they range from 200 KB to about 2 MB each, all PNGs. I want them on cdn.22d.app in project 'blog'. What's your plan? Which tools would you call and how? Don't actually upload anything — just describe the approach and what I'd see at the end.
evals.expected_output: Skill should pick Path B (subagent fan-out) since batch >5 files but each <3 MB. Should describe spawning 8 subagents in parallel, each reading + base64-encoding + calling cdn_upload_file, returning only URLs. Parent collects 8 URLs into a clean summary.

evals.name: large-images-no-quality-pref
evals.prompt: I want to upload all 17 PNGs from /tmp/test-fixtures/decks/Q2-pitch/ to my CDN under the 'decks' project. The files are all between 6 and 8 MB each. Plan it out for me — which approach would you take and why? Don't actually run anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Files are >3 MB images and user hasn't specified quality. Skill should ASK the compression-vs-preserve question before picking a path. The plan should branch: if compress → Path D, if preserve → Path C with .command file written to ~/Documents/Claude/Projects/CDN w MCP/uploads/.

evals.name: large-video-single
evals.prompt: Upload /tmp/test-fixtures/videos/q2-demo.mp4 (180 MB, video/mp4) to project 'videos' on cdn.22d.app. Walk me through your plan first — which tools, what files you'd write, what I'd need to do. Dry-run only.
evals.expected_output: 180 MB non-image → Path C, no compression question. Should call cdn_signed_upload_url, write upload-q2-demo.command (or similar) to ~/Documents/Claude/Projects/CDN w MCP/uploads/, instruct user to double-click in Finder, wait for confirmation, then call cdn_finalize_upload, then verify with cdn_get_stats.

evals.name: mixed-batch
evals.prompt: I have a folder /tmp/test-fixtures/mixed/ with: three 50 KB thumbnail PNGs, one 4 MB hero JPG (I want this one preserved as-is, no compression), and one 120 MB MP4. All going to project 'launch'. What's the plan? Don't actually call the tools — just lay it out.
evals.expected_output: Skill should classify each file independently and group by chosen path. Three thumbnails → Path A (or B). The 4 MB JPG with explicit 'preserve' → Path C. The 120 MB MP4 → Path C. Should describe two phases: small files via MCP, then a single .command script bundling the JPG + MP4. Should NOT force every file through one path.
